verses.1: James, a bond-servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, To the twelve tribes who are dispersed abroad: Greetings.
verses.2: Consider it all joy, my brothers and sisters, when you encounter various trials,
verses.3: knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
verses.4: And let endurance have its perfect result, so that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.
verses.5: But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all generously and without reproach, and it will be given to him.
verses.6: But he must ask in faith without any doubting, for the one who doubts is like the surf of the sea, driven and tossed by the wind.
verses.7: For that person ought not to expect that he will receive anything from the Lord,
verses.8: being a double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.
verses.9: Now the brother or sister of humble circumstances is to glory in his high position;
verses.10: but the rich person is to glory in his humiliation, because like flowering grass he will pass away.
verses.11: For the sun rises with its scorching heat and withers the grass; and its flower falls off and the beauty of its appearance is destroyed; so also the rich person, in the midst of his pursuits, will die out.
verses.12: Blessed is a man who perseveres under trial; for once he has been approved, he will receive the crown of life which the Lord has promised to those who love Him.
verses.13: No one is to say when he is tempted, “I am being tempted by God”; for God cannot be tempted by evil, and He Himself does not tempt anyone.
verses.14: But each one is tempted when he is carried away and enticed by his own lust.
verses.15: Then when lust has conceived, it gives birth to sin; and sin, when it has run its course, brings forth death.
verses.16: Do not be deceived, my beloved brothers and sisters.
verses.17: Every good thing given and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom there is no variation or shifting shadow.
verses.18: In the exercise of His will He gave us birth by the word of truth, so that we would be a kind of first fruits among His creatures.
verses.19: You know this, my beloved brothers and sisters. Now everyone must be quick to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger;
verses.20: for a man’s anger does not bring about the righteousness of God.
verses.21: Therefore, ridding yourselves of all filthiness and all that remains of wickedness, in humility receive the word implanted, which is able to save your souls.
verses.22: But prove yourselves doers of the word, and not just hearers who deceive themselves.
verses.23: For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man who looks at his natural face in a mirror;
verses.24: for once he has looked at himself and gone away, he has immediately forgotten what kind of person he was.
verses.25: But one who has looked intently at the perfect law, the law of freedom, and has continued in it, not having become a forgetful hearer but an active doer, this person will be blessed in what he does.
verses.26: If anyone thinks himself to be religious, yet does not bridle his tongue but deceives his own heart, this person’s religion is worthless.
verses.27: Pure and undefiled religion in the sight of our God and Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their distress, and to keep oneself unstained by the world.
